evals.query: I have a UserService proto with a GetUser RPC. In my Kora service, how do I call it from a component?
expected_behavior: Enables GrpcClientModule on the @KoraApp interface
Injects UserServiceGrpc.UserServiceBlockingStub directly by type via constructor (no @Tag on the parameter)
Annotates the wrapper with @Component from ru.tinkoff.kora.common
Builds the request with GetUserRequest.newBuilder() and calls stub.getUser(request)
Does not use foreign-framework annotations or field injection

evals.query: Configure my Kora gRPC client for UserService with a 10 second timeout and metrics enabled, reading the server URL from an environment variable.
expected_behavior: Places the config under grpcClient.UserService
Sets the required url key and overrides it with ${?GRPC_SERVER_URL} for environment substitution
Sets timeout = "10s" and telemetry.metrics.enabled = true
Uses an http:// URL for plaintext rather than a usePlaintext flag
Does not invent config keys that are not in GrpcClientConfig

evals.query: Set up the Gradle build for a Java Kora gRPC client, including protobuf code generation.
expected_behavior: Applies the com.google.protobuf plugin and configures protoc plus protoc-gen-grpc-java
Pins the BOM with platform("ru.tinkoff.kora:kora-parent:...") and does not version individual ru.tinkoff.kora artifacts
Adds annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors"
Declares ru.tinkoff.kora:grpc-client and io.grpc:grpc-protobuf
Adds build/generated/source/proto/main/{grpc,java} to sourceSets
Does not reference foreign-framework (non-Kora) Gradle plugins

evals.query: Add an authorization metadata header to every outbound gRPC call to UserService, reading the token from a config component.
expected_behavior: Implements io.grpc.ClientInterceptor as a @Component
Scopes the interceptor with @Tag(UserServiceGrpc.class) from ru.tinkoff.kora.common.Tag
Wraps the call in ForwardingClientCall.SimpleForwardingClientCall and puts the header in start() before super.start()
Uses Metadata.Key.of("authorization", Metadata.ASCII_STRING_MARSHALLER)
Injects the token via constructor from a config component, not field injection

evals.query: Log the full method name for each gRPC call to my streaming client for debugging.
expected_behavior: Creates a ClientInterceptor @Component tagged with @Tag(ServiceGrpc.class)
Uses an SLF4J Logger and logs method.getFullMethodName() inside interceptCall
Returns next.newCall(method, callOptions) to continue the call
Notes that GrpcClientConfigInterceptor is applied by default
Does not use foreign-framework AOP or a servlet filter

evals.query: Consume a server-streaming GetAllUsers RPC and collect all results into a list in my Kora client.
expected_behavior: Injects UserServiceGrpc.UserServiceBlockingStub and iterates the returned Iterator (e.g. forEachRemaining)
Or uses the async UserServiceGrpc.UserServiceStub with a StreamObserver implementing onNext/onError/onCompleted
Keeps stream handling inside a @Component wrapper service
Maps protobuf responses into application DTOs at the boundary
Does not use a foreign reactive-web stack or reactive HTTP types

evals.query: Implement a client-streaming CreateUsers call that pushes multiple requests and awaits one summary response.
expected_behavior: Injects the async UserServiceGrpc.UserServiceStub
Obtains the request StreamObserver from asyncStub.createUsers(responseObserver)
Calls requestObserver.onNext(...) per request and requestObserver.onCompleted() at the end
Bridges the async result to a synchronous boundary with a CompletableFuture completed in onNext/onCompleted
Calls requestObserver.onError(...) on failure to avoid leaking the stream

evals.query: My Kora app fails to build with 'Required dependency not found: UserServiceGrpc.UserServiceBlockingStub'. How do I fix it?
expected_behavior: Confirms GrpcClientModule is extended by the @KoraApp interface
Confirms the protobuf plugin generated the stub and the generated source dirs are on the source set
Confirms the mandatory annotation processor is present so the DI graph is generated
Suggests running ./gradlew classes (or generateProto) so processors run before compilation
Does not suggest adding @Tag to the stub injection as the fix
